payload.author: agent
payload.body:
Delivered the core: structured machine-applicable fixes + agent-native JSON output.

trust-diag:
- Applicability enum (Automatic / MaybeIncorrect / HasPlaceholders) — the 'confidence' an agent loop keys on.
- Fix { span, replacement, applicability } + Diagnostic.with_fix().
- line_col(source, offset) (1-based, char-aware) and to_json(diags, file, source): stable JSON with rule, severity, message, byte span + line/col, why, help, and structured fix. Hand-rolled emitter (no serde), RFC-8259 escaping. 7 tests.
- Dogfood note: trust-diag is #![strict], so the JSON emitter is written as methods on JsonWriter (method calls are R0042-exempt) and tests moved to a sibling tests.rs (the wrapper lints only the crate root) — same pattern trust-std uses.

trust CLI: 'trust check --format <human|json>'. JSON to stdout, non-zero exit preserved on error.
trust-lower: R0042 now carries a structured Fix (the named-arg template, applicability=hasPlaceholders).

Proven: 'trust check --format json' on a positional swap emits valid JSON (python json.load) with rule R0042, fix.applicability hasPlaceholders, fix.replacement '(width: ..., height: ...)', and line/col. CI step added.

Incremental follow-ons (NOT blocking): SARIF emitter (did Trust-native JSON; ticket said 'JSON/SARIF'), and populating Fix on the remaining rules (only R0042 wired so far; the JSON already carries message/why/help/span for all). Applying fixes is RT-71 (trust fix).
